quoteLast: on the 23rd instant, I proceeded with my female   tank ‘Mabel’ (No. 9382) in front of the infantry. I made a very   zigzag course to the wood in the south-west edge of the village   of Chuignolles, where I encountered an anti-tank gun which was   eventually knocked out by the male tank commanded by 2nd Lieut.   Simmonds, who was operating on my right. I then worked up the   south side of the village, heavily machine-gunning all the crops   and copses, and dislodged several machine-gun crews of the enemy.
